commentary: Ten sessions versus thirteen last week, so yes, the volume dropped, but let's talk about what actually matters: that Saturday half marathon came in at 159 average heart rate compared to last week's 151 over the exact same distance, which means he went harder, not softer. The swimming held steady at three solid pool sessions and he even added fifty metres on Monday, which Himbo noticed and is being obnoxious about. The weight training got more intentional this week — fewer sessions but hitting legs and back on Sunday after a run the day before, which is either very brave or very stupid, and either way it's extremely him. The one thing worth eyeing is that Wednesday disappeared entirely; last week he was in the pool and doing PT on a Wednesday, and this week it was just a rest day, so if that becomes a pattern it's worth a conversation, but one week does not a habit make.
metrics_commentary: Okay so 6050 metres in the pool and nearly 16 km on your feet — your body is absolutely putting in the work this week, and honestly it shows, which is the whole point. A VO2 Max of 56.1 is genuinely impressive, like that is a fit person's number, and you should let yourself feel smug about it for at least a full day. The HRV sitting at 55 against a seven-day average of 58 is the one thing worth watching — not alarming, but your nervous system is telling you it's carrying something, so sleep, water, maybe a night where you're not trying to prove anything. Two run sessions only is fine this week, but next week we're building that back out, because those legs are wasted sitting still.
gym_commentary: Five sessions when Scott only asked for four — honestly, that's very on-brand, and nobody is complaining. The bench press PR is the headline this week: 31.7 kg estimated one-rep max up from 28.5 kg is not a small jump, that's a chest that is visibly deciding to show up and be noticed. Weekly volume climbed from 22,062 kg to 23,660 kg, which means the body is handling more and asking for more, and that upward trend across quad, hinge, back, and chest strength tells the same story — this is not a plateau, this is a build. The becoming is very much in progress.
